reasoning: On February 7, 2025, the U.S. Board on Geographic Names (BGN) officially renamed the 'Gulf of Mexico' to the 'Gulf of America' following an executive order. Major digital mapping services (Google Maps), federal agencies (USGS, Coast Guard), and reference sites (Britannica) updated their labels for U.S. users shortly thereafter. Crucially, WorldAtlas has already updated its dedicated 'Gulf Of Mexico' article title to include '(Gulf of America)'. Given that the resolution criteria only require the name to appear (including as a secondary name) on the North America map page, and that WorldAtlas is a U.S.-based site that follows official BGN nomenclature, it is highly probable that the site-wide map updates will be completed by the July 1, 2025 deadline. The 5-month window from the official BGN change to the resolution date is sufficient for a major cartographic website to update its primary map graphics.
